relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Joseph prepared the final test with grain and hidden silver.
The cup was placed with Benjamin.
Morning sent the brothers away.
Joseph sent pursuit after them.
The cup became the accusation.
The steward caught them with Joseph’s words.
They rejected the charge.
They argued from their returned silver.
They spoke too strongly, not knowing the cup was with Benjamin.
The steward narrowed the penalty to the one found with the cup.
Every sack was lowered and opened.
The cup was found with Benjamin.
They tore their garments and returned together.
Judah and his brothers fell before Joseph.
Joseph pressed the test.
Judah confessed that guilt had found them.
Joseph offered to keep only Benjamin.
Judah came near and began to intercede.
He recalled Joseph’s question about father and brother.
He described Benjamin as beloved remnant of his mother.
He recalled the command to bring him down.
He named the bond between boy and father.
He remembered the closed face without Benjamin.
He reported everything to Jacob.
Jacob had asked them to return for food.
They could not return without Benjamin.
Jacob remembered Rachel’s two sons.
He remembered Joseph as torn away.
He feared Benjamin’s loss would send him to Sheol in sorrow.
Judah named the soul-bond between father and son.
He saw that Jacob would not survive another loss.
Judah had made himself surety.
Judah offered himself instead of Benjamin.
He could not face his father without the boy.
